title: Vitamin d receptor antagonists and uses thereof
abstract: Compound having the following formula (I), wherein the groups R 1 and R 2 represent independently a functional group selected among a hydrogen and an aliphatic chain; R 3 represents a functional group selected among a hydrogen and a methyl; - R 5 represents a functional group selected among a hydrogen, a methyl and a methylene; - R 6 represents a functional group selected among a hydrogen, methyl, methylene, ethyl and a ethylene; and - wherein the group Q is a carborane substituent having the following molecular formula : C 2 B 10 H 11 R 7 ; wherein R 7 represents a functional group selected among a hydrogen and an aliphatic chain; or a pharmaceutical acceptable derivative thereof, for use as a medicament.
claims:
Claims 1. Compound having the following formula (I) : - wherein the groups Ri and R 2 represent independently a functional group selected among a hydrogen and an aliphatic chain being at least selected among one of the following: linear, branched, cyclic, substituted and unsubstituted chain; the aliphatic chain being selected among Ci-i2alkyl; C2-i2alkenyl; C 2 -i 2 alkynyl; Ci. i2heteroalkyl; C2-i2heteroalkenyl; C2-i2heteroalkynyl; Co- wary I and C4-wheteroaryl group; each of the foregoing alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, heteroalkyl, heteroalkenyl, heteroal kynyl, aryl and heteroaryl group, optionally bearing one or more substituents comprising at least one of the atom or function selected from -F; -Cl; - Br; -I; -NO 2 ; -CN; -CF 3 ; -OH ; -O-; -S-; -C(=O)-: -S(=O)-; -SO 2 -; -SO 2 ; -C(=O)O- and -C(=O)N-; preferably a hydrogen; - R3 represents a functional group selected among a hydrogen and a methyl; preferably a hydrogen; - R5 represents a functional group selected among a hydrogen, a methyl and a methylene; preferably a methylene; - Re represents a functional group selected among a hydrogen, methyl, methylene, ethyl and an ethylene; preferably a hydrogen; and - wherein the group Q is a carborane substituent having the following molecular formula : C2B10H11R7; wherein R7 represents a functional group selected among a hydrogen and an aliphatic chain being at least selected among one of the following: 

 linear, branched, cyclic, substituted and unsubstituted chain; the aliphatic chain being selected among Ci-^alkyl; C2-i2alkenyl; C2-i2alkynyl; Ci.^heteroalkyl; C2- i2heteroalkenyl; C2-i2heteroalkynyl; Ce- aryl and C4-ioheteroaryl group; each of the foregoing alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, heteroalkyl, heteroalkenyl, heteroalkynyl, aryl and heteroaryl group, optionally bearing one or more substituents comprising at least one of the atom or function selected from -F; -Cl; -Br; -I; -NO2; -CN; -CF3; -OH ; -O-; -S-; -C(=O)-; -S(=O)-; -SO2-; -SO 2 ; -C(=O)O- and -C(=O)N-; and preferably a hydrogen; or a pharmaceutical acceptable derivative thereof, for use as a medicament. 2. Compound for its use according to claim 1 , wherein the group Q is selected among one of the following groups numbered from (II) to (IV): , and preferably the group Q is the substituent (II). 3. Compound for its use according to any of claim 1 or 2, wherein is the compound (I) selected among one of the following compounds of formula (1.1 ); (1.2) and (1.3) : 4. Compound according to any of claims 1 to 3, or a pharmaceutical acceptable derivative thereof, for use in the treatment of hypercalcemia disorders of a subject presenting acute or chronic hypercalcemia. 5. Compound according to any of claims 1 to 3, or a pharmaceutical acceptable derivative thereof, for use in the treatment of acute or chronic hypercalciuria. 6. Compound according to any of claims 1 to 5, or a pharmaceutical acceptable derivative thereof, for use in the treatment of hypercalcemia disorders of a subject presenting acute or chronic hypercalcemia and/or chronic hypercalciuria together with low PTH levels. 7. Compound according to claim 6, or a pharmaceutical acceptable derivative thereof, for use in the treatment of a subject suffering from at least one of the following among subcutaneous fat necrosis ; idiopathic infantile hypercalcemia ; and Williams syndrome. 8. Compound according to any of claims 1 to 7, or a pharmaceutical acceptable derivative thereof, performing the function of reducing the transcript levels of VDR target genes induced by 1 ,25D3. 9. Compound according to claim 8, or a pharmaceutical acceptable derivative thereof, for use as a vitamin D receptor antagonist. 10. Compound according to any of claims 1 to 9, or a pharmaceutical acceptable derivative thereof, for use in the treatment of a non-human mammal.
